design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one fighting with special moves and finishing moves triggered by specific strike patterns
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learning opponent patterns and executing precise inputs to land finishing moves
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as NBA star Shaquille O'Neal in martial arts combat across fantastical dimensions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Celebrity athlete in fighting game genre; unusual premise of basketball player in supernatural martial arts narrative; blood code mechanics echo Mortal Kombat design philosophy

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden 'blood code' button sequence unlocks finishing moves, adding hidden depth layer

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode progression through sequential fights; Genesis version has 5 additional playable characters and 3 additional stages compared to SNES version
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with different movesets (7 in SNES, 12 in Genesis)
Hidden blood code for alternate finishing moves

world_building_techniques: Character dialogue with martial arts master Leotsu establishing dojo setting
Inter-dimensional travel plot (Tokyo dojo to 'Second World')
Boss encounter narrative (rescuing boy Nezu from mummy Sett-Ra)
Post-victory consequence (Beast follows protagonist back to real world)

boss_design.philosophy: Final boss Sett-Ra serves as narrative antagonist and primary objective; minions including Beast provide secondary challenges and narrative closure
notable_bosses: Beast (Sett-Ra's minion, appears in both Second World and real world)

faction_indicators: Second World inhabitants vs. real world inhabitants
Opposing basketball team led by Beast
danger_signals: Protagonist mistaken for 'warrior chosen by the Stars' indicating external threat
Interdimensional invasion (Beast crossing into real world)
environmental_storytelling: Dojo setting establishes martial arts context
Second World dimensions represent otherworldly threat
Basketball game venue as contrast to supernatural conflict
